mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs compress equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of employment growth and rising unemployment creates a recessionary feedback loop that suppresses consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial output, supply bottlenecks support corporate pricing power and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns over 60 days are not positively correlated with INDPRO levels when industrial production remains below 110, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during demand-shock recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals labor market resilience, which supports consumer-driven earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following payroll increases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,500 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and broader economic sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of months where HOUST drops below 1,500, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in consumer credit balances (RSAFS) and elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates unsustainable debt servicing pressures that will trigger a credit contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and FEDFUNDS > 4.5% fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, a sequential rise in real GDP (GDPC1) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals sustainable demand normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns over 5 days are not positively correlated with rising GDPC1 when CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5% month-over-month, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during stagflationary episodes.
